original_abstract:
Regulation (EU) 2017/746 on in vitro diagnostic medical devices (IVDR) introduces stringent requirements for pathology departments that develop and utilize in-house in vitro diagnostic medical devices (IH-IVDs). While the regulation officially took effect on 26 May 2022, an amending regulation from the European Parliament and the Council has established a phased implementation period for many of these conditions. However, compliance with the general safety and performance requirements outlined in Annex I became mandatory immediately upon the IVDR’s entry into force.

In this article, we detail the practical steps taken at the Institute of Pathology at the University Hospital of Heidelberg to implement the currently applicable IVDR mandates. By sharing our institutional experience, we aim to provide a roadmap and practical assistance for other healthcare institutions facing similar regulatory transitions.

Our findings suggest that while implementing these requirements is an intensive process, several guidance documents and academic handouts have become available to help harmonize the interpretation of Article 5 (5) for healthcare institutions. Furthermore, active participation in academic networks is essential for navigating the complexities of the IVDR. For both university and non-university pathology departments, achieving IVDR conformity represents a significant burden on personnel and time. This regulatory pressure adds to the existing challenges of patient care, teaching, research, and the continuous evolution of quality management systems required for modern precision diagnostics.
humanized_abstract:
IVDR and its consequences for laboratories; new regulations in Europe The new European regulation IVDR (EU) 2017/746 relating to in vitro diagnostic medical devices (IVD) has a significant impact on pathology laboratories with inhouse production of IVDs. The new EU regulation came into force on 26th of May 2022. In the meantime, a modification to the IVDR was decided upon in Europe by European Parliament and Council; this modification will cause individual sections to be implemented over time. Nonetheless, as regards compliance with the General Safety and Performance Requirements, mentioned in Annex I, this entered in force on 26th of May 2022 upon implementation of the IVDR.

In this article, we describe step by step how the implementation of current IVDR regulations (In Vitro Diagnostic Regulation) is being proceeded in the context of the Institute of Pathology at University Hospital of Heidelberg. Our intention is to support other laboratories by providing a documented example of how challenges arising from a regulatory change can be addressed practically.

Implementing Article 5 (5) is a huge effort, but we managed to find several guidelines and lecture papers that can help to harmonize the understanding of Article 5 (5) in hospitals. Moreover, being part of scientific networks is necessary to understand the IVDR in more detail. Our pathologies laboratory at the university, as well as the non-university pathology laboratories have to make a huge effort on the IVDR to obtain the compliance. This is a high workload and time effort on staff which heavily impacts on patient care, teaching and research activities, as well as quality management in laboratories performing highly advanced precision diagnostics.
